blocks.content:
Snapped at Fatima about Chen prior art search. She didn't deserve that. I'm not sleeping and it's showing.

She came in with her research and I immediately jumped on the fact that she hadn't pulled the continuation patents. The look on her face. She's 29 and she's trying so hard and I made her feel small in front of the team. I need to fix this tomorrow. And I need to actually sleep.

January budget update

Chase Sapphire balance: $4,200 (up from $2,900 pre-holidays)
Capital One checking 8234: $1,487 after tuition payment
Emergency fund 5691: $14,200 (untouched but not growing)
529 for Renata: $8,500

Tuition restart hit hard this month. Holiday spending plus the regular bills. Need to cut discretionary for the next two months or the emergency fund stays frozen.

Study abroad update

Renata told me tonight the Valencia program is $8,500 on top of tuition. $2,000 deposit due Jan 10, rest due Feb 1.

529 has exactly $8,500 in it. That was supposed to last through senior year.

Emergency fund: $14,200. If I pull from savings to cover this plus keep paying tuition, I'm looking at maybe $12,800 by end of month after November bills.

Need to call David Chen about whether to touch the 529 or pay out of pocket. Also going to ask Tomas to split it. Not optimistic.

If I do this I won't hit my $20K emergency fund target until 2027.

Thanksgiving Special -- Pasteles de Mama

Recipes:
- Pasteles (main): Mama's recipe with banana leaves, masa, pork filling
- Arroz con gandules: classic PR rice
- Turkey: herb-rubbed, garlic butter

Shot list:
1. Intro at kitchen counter
2. Masa preparation close-up
3. Filling the banana leaves -- overhead
4. Tying the pasteles -- step by step
5. Boiling pot -- steam rising
6. Arroz con gandules prep -- sofrito, gandules, olives
7. Turkey check -- oven shot
8. Final spread -- table set
9. Taste test
10. Outro -- Feliz Thanksgiving

Ingredient budget: $180
- Banana leaves (2 packs): $12
- Pork shoulder (5 lbs): $28
- Masa ingredients (yuca, plantains, calabaza): $35
- Gandules canned (6): $15
- Sofrito ingredients: $12
- Rice (10 lbs): $18
- Turkey (14 lbs): $35
- Herbs, garlic, butter, olives, capers: $25

Posting schedule:
- Film: Sat Nov 22 (prep) + Thu Nov 27 (cooking)
- Edit: Nov 28 evening
- Upload: Nov 28 night
- YouTube premiere: Nov 29 morning
- Instagram reels: 3 clips Nov 29 - Dec 1

Note: Renata here for Thanksgiving but doesnt know about the channel. Film around her schedule. Calendar blocks labeled "home project."

Brand Inquiry -- CocinaTu Kitchen

Received: Dec 2, 2025
Contact: partnerships@cocinatu.com
Offer: Holiday recipe video collaboration
They send: Abuela Collection cookware set (retail ~$379)
Payment: $500 for one sponsored video
Requirements: Mention products, link in description, post by Dec 20

PROS:
- First real brand deal
- Free cookware (I actually need new pots)
- $500 is $500
- Latin-owned brand -- aligns with my content
- Small brand, probably wont attract attention

CONS:
- Brand deal = tax implications, need to report as income
- Contract with my name linking me to @AdriCocina
- If anyone at the firm googles CocinaTu and finds the video
- Sponsorships mean the channel stops being just a hobby
- Dec 20 deadline during busiest work month
- What if David finds out about a side business?

Decision: Not now. Maybe spring after Meridian trial. Respond politely, say timing doesnt work, stay in touch.

Assigned Fatima the Claim 3/7 dependency analysis today. She was ecstatic -- practically glowing when I told the team she'd be taking lead.

But I keep going back to Claim 7. The '512 continuation has a narrowing amendment that Chen will absolutely use for prosecution history estoppel. I told Fatima I was confident in the dependency chain. I need to look at it again because I'm not sure Claim 7 survives if Chen's counsel is halfway competent.

I wasn't sure. And I sounded sure. That's the part that bothers me.

Three questions today. Three confident answers. Zero certainty.

Grayson asked about the damages model -- GP vs analytical. I said GP like it was the only option. I changed my mind three times this week. But I can't say that in front of the team. They need someone who knows.

Fatima's dependency analysis is actually getting good. Better than I expected. She found an estoppel angle I hadn't fully mapped. Which should make me proud but instead I keep thinking: what if she figures out I'm not as sure as I sound?

I need to stop writing at midnight.

Fatima's Markman brief draft landed today. It's good. Really good. Clean claim construction, thorough prosecution history, smart use of the Vitronics framework.

Did I teach her that or did she figure it out despite me? I keep looking for the holes and there aren't many. Grayson said it was 'first-chair quality' and he wasn't wrong.

I think I feel relieved. And a little bit like a fraud. Like I've been performing certainty for two months and she just... did the work.

I should tell her it's good. I did tell her it's good. In writing, in front of the team. That part I can do.

Cayenne brake emergency

Porsche of Tysons Corner estimate: $3,400
- Front and rear rotors + pads, both axles
- Grinding noise started last week, cant avoid this

Payment:
- Chase Sapphire 4417: $1,800 (card balance now $4,700)
- Capital One checking 8234: $1,600 (checking balance ~$4,200)

Emergency fund 5691: drops to ~$9,600

Car in shop 2-3 days. Ubering to work or getting rides.

This on top of $4,300 tuition and the study abroad payments. I cannot keep absorbing hits like this.
Living Room Built-in Bookshelf
South wall of living room, flanking the window. Built-in style, anchored to wall with french cleats.
Dimensions

Overall: 84" H x 72" W x 12" D
Two sections, each 36" wide
5 adjustable shelves per section, 12" spacing
Base cabinet 18" H with doors
Top trim piece to meet ceiling (crown molding later maybe)
Materials

3/4" birch plywood (2 sheets) for shelves and sides
1/4" birch plywood for back panels
1x2 poplar for face frames
Kreg pocket hole screws 2.5"
Shelf pins 5mm brass
Minwax Provincial stain #211
Minwax Polycrylic satin finish
Titebond III glue
French cleats for wall mounting

Need stud finder before install. Studs should be 16" OC on south wall but check.
Do all cutting in basement. Sand everything to 220 before staining.
Two coats stain, three coats poly minimum.

Dia de los Muertos 2025

Menu:
- Tamales: pork green chile + chicken mole (double batch)
- Pan de muerto
- Champurrado
- Mole negro (small batch for ofrenda)
- Arroz con leche

Ofrenda: Mama's favorites -- tres leches slice, cafe con leche, arepa with queso fresco

Shopping list -- Eastern Market (Sat Oct 25):
- Corn husks (2 bags)
- Pork shoulder (4 lbs)
- Tomatillos (2 lbs)
- Chiles: ancho, guajillo, pasilla
- Mexican chocolate (Abuelita, 3 tablets)
- Piloncillo (2 cones)
- Masa harina (5 lb bag)
- Manteca/lard (2 lbs)
- Marigolds (check garden first)

Already have: rice, cinnamon sticks, condensed milk, vanilla

H Mart backup if Eastern Market doesnt have banana leaves or dried shrimp

Cooking schedule:
Fri Oct 31 evening: make tamales (4-5 hr process)
Sat Nov 1: pan de muerto, champurrado, arroz con leche
Set up ofrenda Saturday afternoon

Thanksgiving 2025 -- Cooking Schedule

Dinner at 4 PM Thursday.

Menu:
- Turkey (14 lbs) herb-rubbed garlic butter
- Arroz con gandules
- Pasteles (pork, banana leaf)
- Tres leches cake (cinnamon version)
- Cranberry sauce w/ orange zest
- Sweet potato casserole
- Rolls from Bread Furst

Grocery status:
[x] Amazon Fresh delivery Mon Nov 24
[x] Spices + pantry staples -- good
[ ] Banana leaves + plantains -- H Mart before Wed
[ ] Rolls -- Bread Furst pickup Wed AM

Schedule:
Tue night: brine turkey
Wed morning: pasteles (masa, filling, wrap)
Wed afternoon: tres leches, cranberry sauce, prep sweet potatoes
Thu 10 AM: turkey in at 325
Thu 2 PM: arroz con gandules
Thu 3 PM: reheat pasteles
Thu 3:30: casserole in oven
Thu 3:45: warm rolls
Thu 4 PM: eat

Budget: ~$159 main order + $25 specialty items + $12 bread = ~$196 total
Shenandoah Trip Planning -- March 14-15

Trails
- Old Rag Summit Loop (9.1 mi / 14.5 km) -- rocky scramble, start EARLY, parking fills by 8 AM
- Dark Hollow Falls (1.4 mi round trip) -- easy morning walk, good for day 2
- Stony Man Trail (1.6 mi) -- sunset option if we have energy
- Whiteoak Canyon (9.2 mi) -- backup if Old Rag too crowded

Gear Checklist
[x] Hiking boots (broken in)
[x] Water bottles + Sawyer filter
[x] Headlamps (Sol has new one)
[x] First aid kit
[x] Layers -- fleece + rain shell
[x] Sleeping bags x2
[x] Tent (2 person)
[x] Camp stove + french press (Sol bringing stove)
[x] Sunscreen
[x] Trail snacks (Clif bars + Sol's trail mix)
[ ] Cooler with ice

Campsite
Booked: Big Meadows Campground, Site B-47
Reservation #REC-38742961
Check-in after 1:00 PM
Check-out by 12:00 PM
Cost: $30/night + $30 Skyline Drive entrance = $60 total

Food (Sol handling)
- Chicken empanadas for trail
- Dark chocolate trail mix
- Coffee + camp stove
- I'm bringing: Clif bars, apples, water

Schedule
Sat Mar 14: Leave DC 5:30 AM, pick up Sol at 5. Trailhead by 7. Old Rag hike (5-6 hrs). Set up camp at Big Meadows.
Sun Mar 15: Easy morning walk (Dark Hollow Falls). Break camp by noon. Drive home.

Tax Prep 2025

Documents:
[x] W-2 -- Kendrick Sloane via ADP (received Feb 1)
[x] 1098 -- Capital One mortgage interest (received Jan 28, $18,742)
[x] 1098-T -- UVA for Renata (forwarded Feb 5, $43,000)
[x] 1099-DIV -- Fidelity (downloaded Feb 14, $412 dividends)
[x] 1099 -- Google (ad revenue, $720)
[x] 1099-INT -- Capital One savings ($187 interest)

CPA: Patricia Reeves, (202) 555-0641
Appointment: Feb 20, 11 AM -- DONE
Follow-up call: Mar 10, 2 PM

Questions for Patricia:
[x] Education credits for Renata (AOTC confirmed, 2 years remaining)
[x] Study abroad tuition counts (billed through UVA, qualifies)
[x] Ad revenue -- Schedule C, deducted internet + equipment
[ ] Estimated quarterly payments for 2026?

Draft return received Mar 1:
- Federal refund: $2,572
- DC refund: $559
- AOTC: $2,500
- Review before Mar 10 call

Spring Garden 2026 -- Raised Bed Plan

Backyard layout:
- Bed 1 (4x8 ft, south wall): Tomatoes (Big Boy) + basil (companion planting, supposed to help with pests)
- Bed 2 (4x4 ft, east side): Peppers -- CalWonder sweet + Serrano hot. Full sun.
- Bed 3 (2x4 ft, near kitchen door): Herbs -- cilantro, extra basil, maybe oregano if theres room. Easy access for cooking.

Planting schedule (DC zone 7a):
- Mar 15: Prep beds -- clear winter debris, turn soil, add new raised bed mix + fertilizer
- Mar 15: Start seeds indoors in greenhouse kits (tomatoes + peppers need 6-8 weeks head start)
- Late Apr: Direct sow cilantro (bolts in heat, succession plant every 3 weeks)
- Mid May (after last frost ~May 10): Transplant tomato + pepper seedlings outside
- May: Plant basil outside next to tomatoes

Soil order (Amazon):
- 3 bags Miracle-Gro raised bed soil
- 2 bags hardwood mulch
- Espoma Garden-Tone fertilizer
- 2 seed starting greenhouse kits

Seed order (Burpee, placed Mar 10):
- Big Boy tomato
- CalWonder sweet pepper
- Serrano hot pepper
- Cilantro
- Genovese basil
- Seed starting mix

Herb priorities for cooking:
1. Cilantro -- sofrito, salsas, garnish for basically everything. #1 priority. Plant a LOT.
2. Basil -- pesto, caprese, Thai basil chicken. Genovese variety.
3. Peppers -- fresh serranos for hot sauce, CalWonder for stuffed peppers and arroz
4. Tomatoes -- BLTs, salsa, caprese, pasta sauce. Need at least 4 plants.

Notes:
- Sol wants fresh herbs for dinner parties. Plant extra cilantro row for her.
- Cilantro next to basil per that article Sol sent (companion planting reduces bolting)
- Check if old tomato cages survived winter -- basement storage
- Last year the basil got leggy, pinch more aggressively this time

Updated Mar 15: Beds cleared out. Old soil looks ok but adding fresh mix on top. Started tomatoes and peppers indoors. Greenhouse kits on kitchen windowsill. Waiting on Amazon delivery for the rest of the soil and mulch.

Need to track these card charges better. Three hits on the Chase card this week I didn't plan for. CapCut renewal $45.99, Amazon $78.50 (ring light + cookbook), Rockler $95 for finishing supplies. That's $219 and change I wasn't counting on. Everything adds up fast when you're not watching it. Should set a calendar reminder for the CapCut renewal next year so it doesn't sneak up again.

Arroz con Gandules -- Spring Comfort Food

This is the one Mama always made on Sundays. Going to do a full from-scratch version.

Recipe:
- Sofrito (recaito base: culantro, ajies, garlic, onion, cilantro)
- Gandules (canned Goya, drain and rinse)
- Sazon with annatto
- Annatto oil (achiote seeds in olive oil)
- Medium grain rice
- Pork -- small cubes of pernil leftovers or salt pork
- Olives, capers, tomato sauce

Side: Tostones (green plantains, double-fried)

Shot list:
1. Intro -- "this is the dish that raised me"
2. Making sofrito from scratch -- food processor close-up
3. Annatto oil prep -- beautiful orange color
4. Browning the pork
5. Building flavor layers (sofrito + sazon + tomato sauce)
6. Adding gandules + olives + rice
7. Low and slow -- lid on, steam rising
8. Tostones prep -- peel, slice, first fry, smash, second fry
9. Final plating -- arroz with tostones on the side
10. Taste test and outro

Filming: Wed Mar 25, afternoon
Ingredients prep: Tue Mar 24 evening

Budget: ~$45
- Gandules (2 cans): $5
- Pork shoulder (2 lbs): $12
- Plantains (6): $6
- Rice (5 lb bag): $8
- Sofrito ingredients: $8
- Sazon + annatto seeds: $6

Post schedule:
- Film Mar 25
- Edit Mar 26-27
- Upload Mar 28
- YouTube premiere: Mar 29
- Instagram reels: 2 clips Mar 29-30

December spending review

Holiday damage:
- Ticketmaster Wizards solo game: $52
- Total Wine (2 orders): ~$90
- Amazon gifts for Renata: ~$180
- Whole Foods holiday groceries: ~$95
- Extra dining out: ~$120
- Renata textbooks forwarded from UVA: $339

Total holiday overspend vs. budget: ~$450 over

Chase Sapphire balance: climbing to ~$4,200
Checking 8234: ~$2,900 after everything
Emergency fund 5691: $14,200 (held steady)
529: $8,500 (untouched, study abroad deposit Jan 10)

Need to cut back in January. The 529 is going to get wiped by study abroad.
Textbooks were a surprise expense -- $339 for 5 books is outrageous.

February budget -- post-Cayenne damage

Porsche brake repair: $3,400
  - $1,800 on Chase Sapphire 4417
  - $1,600 from checking 8234

Chase Sapphire balance now: ~$5,247 (statement came, minimum $125 due Mar 14)
Checking 8234: ~$2,600
Emergency fund 5691: ~$9,600
529: depleted (study abroad paid)

Also this month:
- Tuition installment: $4,300
- Uber rides (car in shop 3 days): ~$85
- Regular bills all hit

Income held steady at $7,700 biweekly but the Cayenne wiped out any breathing room.
Need to pay down the Chase card aggressively in March. Minimum is $125 but targeting $500.

Tax refund coming: ~$3,131 ($2,572 federal + $559 DC). That helps but it's not enough to fix everything.

First month on Meridian feels solid. Discovery is massive -- 400+ docs in the initial production. Grayson is sharp on damages strategy. Fatima is eager but green.

The '847 patent has seven independent claims and Chen's counsel will absolutely go after prosecution history estoppel on Claims 3 and 7. The '512 continuation is the weak spot. Need to map the full amendment history before we get too far into claim construction.

Trial is set for April 14. Six months. That's tight for a case this size but David is confident. I need to be too.

Year-end on Meridian. Discovery is mostly complete. Fatima's prior art research has been thorough -- she found three continuation patents I'd missed in my initial review. Grayson locked in Dr. Reeves for damages.

Privilege log dispute is still simmering. Meet and confer hasn't resolved it. May need to file a motion to compel in January if they keep stonewalling on the internal testing data.

Holiday break coming. Need to step away from this case for a few days. My brain is running claim charts in my sleep.

January content status: paused

Haven't filmed since December. Between work stress, the self-assessment, and not sleeping well, I just don't have the energy to be on camera right now.

Channel stats (Jan):
- Views still trickling in on the Thanksgiving video (~2,400 total)
- 3 new subscribers this month (241 total)
- Ad revenue: ~$52

Ideas for when I come back:
- Arroz con gandules (Mama's Sunday recipe)
- Quick weeknight sofrito
- Tostones tutorial (always gets engagement)

Sol keeps asking when the next video is. Told her "soon." Not sure when soon is.
